name: bw2parameters

metadata.name: bw2parameters
metadata.summary: Contains all the python boilerplate you need to create a Brightway ecosystem package.

documentation:
# Brightway2 parameters

Library for storing, validating, and calculating with parameters. Designed to work with the [Brightway2 life cycle assessment framework](https://brightwaylca.org), but is generic enough to work in other use cases.

    In [1]: from bw2parameters import ParameterSet

    In [2]: parameters = {
       ...:        'Deep_Thought': {'amount': 42},
       ...:        'East_River_Creature': {'formula': '2 * Deep_Thought + 16'},
       ...:        'Elders_of_Krikkit': {'formula': 'sqrt(East_River_Creature)'},
       ...: }

    In [3]: ParameterSet(parameters).evaluate()
    Out[3]: {'Deep_Thought': 42, 'East_River_Creature': 100, 'Elders_of_Krikkit': 10.0}

Compatible with Python 2.7 and 3.3+. 100% test coverage. [Source code on Github](https://github.com/brightway-lca/brightway2-parameters), documentation on [Read the Docs](https://brightway2-parameters.readthedocs.io/).

classes.name: bw2parameters.errors.ValidationError
documentation: Base class for errors with variables and formulas

classes.name: bw2parameters.errors.CapitalizationError
documentation: Parameter(s) names are case-sensitive

classes.name: bw2parameters.errors.DuplicateName
documentation: This variable name has already been defined elsewhere

classes.name: bw2parameters.errors.MissingName
documentation: Formula refers to a variable which is not defined

classes.name: bw2parameters.errors.SelfReference
documentation: Formula refers to itself

classes.name: bw2parameters.errors.BroadcastingError
documentation: Formula returns Monte Carlo results with wrong dimensions

modules.name: docs.conf
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
This file is execfile()d with the current directory set to its containing dir.
Note that not all possible configuration values are present in this
autogenerated file.
All configuration values have a default; values that are commented out
serve to show the default.

functions.name: bw2parameters.utils.isidentifier
documentation:
Determines, if string is valid Python identifier.

Stolen from http://stackoverflow.com/questions/12700893/how-to-check-if-a-string-is-a-valid-python-identifier-including-keyword-check

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.Interpreter.get_symbols
documentation: Parses an expression and returns all symbols.

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.Interpreter.get_unknown_symbols
documentation: Parses an expression and returns all symbols which are neither in the symtable nor passed via known_symbols.

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.Interpreter.add_symbols
documentation: Adds symbols to the symtable.

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.Interpreter.remove_symbols
documentation: Removes symbols from the symtable.

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.PintInterpreter.get_unknown_symbols
documentation:
Parses the given expression and returns a list of symbols, which are neither contained in the symtable,
nor in known_symbols, nor can be interpreted as pint units

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.PintInterpreter.get_pint_symbols
documentation: Parses an expression and returns all symbols which can be interpreted as pint units.

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.PintInterpreter.add_symbols
documentation:
Adds symbols to symtable while making sure that pint Quantities are from same registry as self.ureg
(otherwise self.eval will fail).

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.PintInterpreter._raise_proper_pint_exception
documentation: Make sure that pint exceptions are correctly raised during evaluation

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.PintInterpreter.parameter_list_to_dict
documentation:
Takes a list of parameter objects and returns a dict where keys are the parameter names and values
are the interpreted pint.Quantities (or float where no unit is defined).

methods.name: bw2parameters.interpreter.PintInterpreter.set_amount_and_unit
documentation:
Takes an arbitrary object and tries to set it's `amount` and `unit` fields. `amount` field is the magnitude of
the pint.Quantity after conversion to `to_unit`.         If no `to_unit` is given, the quantity's own unit will be used. If the input is not a pint.Quantity then
`obj['unit']` will be used. If no quantity is given, then `obj['amount']` and `obj['unit']` are used.

methods.name: bw2parameters.pint.PintWrapperSingleton.to_unit
documentation: Returns pint.Unit if the given string can be interpreted as a unit, returns None otherwise

methods.name: bw2parameters.pint.PintWrapperSingleton.to_units
documentation:
Takes and iterable and tries to interpret each element as a pint.Unit. Returns a dict where key is
the original element and value is the interpreted pint.Unit. Elements which cannot be interpreted as
a pint.Unit are `None` (or dropped if `drop_none == True`).

methods.name: bw2parameters.pint.PintWrapperSingleton.to_quantity
documentation: Return a pint.Quantity if a unit is given, otherwise the amount.

functions.name: bw2parameters.mangling.mangle_formula
documentation:
Add `prefix` to all variable names in formula `string`, except those in `context` or builtin to Python, `math`, or `numpy`.

Uses [asteval](https://newville.github.io/asteval/) and [astunparse](http://astunparse.readthedocs.io/).

Returns the formula as a string.

Example usage:

... code-block:: python

> ```pycon
> >>> mangle_formula("log(foo * bar) + 7 / baz", "pre", ['bar'])
> '(log((pre__foo * bar)) + (7 / pre__baz))'
> ```

functions.name: bw2parameters.mangling.prefix_parameter_dict
documentation:
Add `prefix` to each key in `dct`. Also updates the formulas, if present.

Adds `original` to each value in `dct` with the original key name.

Returns the new dictionary, and a dictionary of name substitutions like `{old: new}`

functions.name: bw2parameters.mangling.substitute_in_formulas
documentation:
Substitute symbol names in `dct` formulas following `substitutions`.

Modifies in place. Returns the modified `dct`.

classes.name: bw2parameters.mangling.PrefixNameAdder
documentation: Change name of all symbols by adding a prefix, unless name already in `context`.

classes.name: bw2parameters.mangling.OnlySelected
documentation: Change name of all symbols already redefined in `substitutes`.

classes.name: bw2parameters.mangling.FormulaSubstitutor
documentation: Callable class that will substitute symbol names using `substitutions` substitution dictionary.

modules.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.get_order
documentation: Get a list of parameter name in an order that they can be safely evaluated

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.get_references
documentation: Create dictionary of parameter references

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.basic_validation
documentation: Basic validation needed to build `references` and `order`

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.evaluate
documentation: Evaluate each formula. Returns dictionary of parameter names and values.

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.evaluate_and_set_amount_field
documentation: Evaluate each formula. Updates the `amount` field of each parameter.

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.evaluate_monte_carlo
documentation:
Evaluate each formula using Monte Carlo and variable uncertainty data, if present.

Formulas **must** return a one-dimensional array, or `BroadcastingError` is raised.

Returns dictionary of `{parameter name: numpy array}`.

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.__call__
documentation: Evaluate each formula, and update `exchanges` if they reference a `parameter` name.

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.ParameterSet.get_interpreter
documentation: Get an instance of `asteval.Interpreter` that is prepopulated with global and local         symbol names and values.

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.PintParameterSet.get_references
documentation: Create dictionary of parameter references

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.PintParameterSet.evaluate
documentation: Evaluate each formula. Returns dictionary of parameter names and values.

methods.name: bw2parameters.parameter_set.PintParameterSet.evaluate_and_set_amount_field
documentation:
Evaluate each formula. Updates the `amount` field of each parameter. Also updates the `unit` field
if no unit is given.
